connection_type: I'm not "involved" with people with disabilities, I AM disabled
issues:
I thought this was supposed to be a survey of people who are disabled. First question and it already seems like I'm being devalued as a person with disabilities.
I'm not "involved" with people with disabilities, I AM disabled. I'm not a "sector" to be involved with, I'm a human being. 
Your glaringly ableist and dismissive attitude shines through, when all you want to hear from is people who are "involved" with us and not us and our opinions, which you devalue.
partners_raw: I don't "collaborate", I'm disabled. There's nobody to "collaborate" with. There's NOBODY who truly advocates for disabled rights in Canada! None of the disability organizations I'm familiar with want to hear from me, this apparently includes you. All they do is step between us and the public and pretend to represent our interests when they're only in it for themselves and they do NOTHING to help us.

connection_type: Advocacy (i.e., civil society organization, disabled persons organization)
issues: I am actively involved in global disability advocacy through the Rotary Club of World Disability Advocacy, with a focus on accessibility, inclusion, equality, stigma reduction, and education. My work spans across various disability areas, including post-polio syndrome, neurodiversity, assistive technology, and inclusive education. I also engage in initiatives that support individuals with acquired disabilities, children with disabilities, and broader community awareness.
partners_raw: 1.	Rotary Club of World Disability Advocacy (RCWDA) – Collaborating with the Board and international members to promote global disability awareness, facilitate inclusive projects, and address accessibility challenges. 	2.	March of Dimes Canada (Post-Polio Syndrome Program) – Working with program leads and participants to raise awareness, provide resources, and advocate for people living with post-polio syndrome. 3.	Global Disability Advocates and Experts – Working with leaders and specialists across multiple countries through RCWDA and related forums to share best practices, raise awareness, and develop solutions for disability inclusion.

connection_type: Advocacy (i.e., civil society organization, disabled persons organization)
issues: The National ME/FM Action Network is a registered charity working on behalf of Canadians with Myalgic Encephalomyelitis/ Chronic Fatigue Syndrome, Fibromyalgia or both. A key impairment with these conditions is a reduction in the overall amount of physical, cognitive and emotional activity people can handle. Generally, people with reduced activity levels cut back their social and recreational activities first, then work or education, then family and household activities, then self-care. Cutting back activities reduces participation in society  Even though our people‚Äôs participation in society is reduced because of long-term impairment, ME and FM were not traditionally considered as disabling. Our people receive both inadequate health care support and inadequate social support.
partners_raw:
We would very much like to collaborate with the federal government and others to implement CRPD rights for Canadians with Myalgic Encephalomyelitis, Fibromyalgia or both. However, we are having great difficulty finding officials and others willing to pay attention to the ME/FM community. This unwillingness is a major barrier.

The questions in this survey assume collaboration. In the absence of collaboration, we do not know how to complete this survey. 

Our story is an important one and we hope it gets attention in your research.
